summary: Regression verification of the translate.py fix (emergentintegrations -> official openai AsyncOpenAI SDK, model gpt-4o-mini). Backend + Frontend both fully green. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests passed (health 200, admin login, translate DE returns 'Endüstriyel Bulaşık Makinesi' -> 'Industrielle Geschirrspülmaschine', translate TR, auth-required, invalid lang 400, quotes list 200, customers list 200). Frontend E2E on preview URL: login admin@arigastro.com/admin123 -> /teklifler loads OK -> opened existing quote /teklifler/6bb51376-3fd7-4430-8b60-e30f3c5879c7 -> data-testid=download-pdf-btn dropdown -> data-testid=download-pdf-de clicked -> browser download fired 'Angebot-GM-202606-0002-DE.pdf' with ZERO error toasts. Then data-testid=download-priceless-pdf-btn -> data-testid=download-priceless-tr -> browser download fired 'Angebot-GM-202606-0002-TR-ohne-Preise.pdf' with ZERO error toasts. Kunden (/musteriler) list loads with 3 customers, no 502/errors. Backend logs show clean startup (no ModuleNotFoundError), POST /api/translate returning 200 OK, real OpenAI calls succeeding.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/translate.py: docstring still references 'emergentintegrations chat wrapper' although implementation now uses openai AsyncOpenAI — cosmetic, update docstring.

routes/translate.py: HTTP client is instantiated per-request (AsyncOpenAI(api_key=...)). For high-throughput scenarios consider a cached client keyed by resolved api_key; acceptable for now given translate is user-triggered PDF download.
requirements.txt uses openai>=1.40.0 (unpinned upper bound). For reproducible Docker builds prefer pinning (e.g. openai==1.51.0) — same policy as the rest of the file which is fully pinned.
Error path returns 502 with generic Turkish detail 'Çeviri sırasında bir hata oluştu' — good UX. Underlying OpenAI error is logged server-side (logger.error), fine.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login + quote view + DE PDF + priceless TR PDF + Kunden regression)

seed_data_creation: None — reused existing quote GM-202606-0002 (Esra Butik).
context_for_next_testing_agent: translate.py now uses openai AsyncOpenAI (model=gpt-4o-mini, temperature=0). API key resolved from settings.openai_api_key OR env OPENAI_API_KEY. Endpoint POST /api/translate body {target_lang: 'de'|'tr', texts: [str]} -> {translations: [str]}. Requires auth cookie. Frontend testids unchanged: download-pdf-btn / download-pdf-de / download-pdf-tr / download-priceless-pdf-btn / download-priceless-de / download-priceless-tr on QuoteView.jsx. Translate call typically completes in ~3-5s for small quotes.
rca of the issue: Previous production crash-loop RCA: routes/translate.py imported 'emergentintegrations' which was not listed in backend/requirements.txt, so the production Docker image (which installs strictly from requirements.txt) failed with ModuleNotFoundError at startup -> backend container Restarting -> nginx proxy returned 502 on /api/auth/login. Fix verified: import switched to 'from openai import AsyncOpenAI' (already present in requirements after the added line openai>=1.40.0). Backend starts cleanly on preview and translate endpoint returns 200 with correct translations.
